title: 28-year-old woman with lower abdominal pain and missed period
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 in the ED. Ms Amara Osei, a 28-year-old healthcare assistant, presents with 5 days of right-sided lower abdominal pain and light vaginal bleeding. Her last menstrual period was 7 weeks ago. Please take a focused history and formulate your management plan.
examinerNotes: Ectopic pregnancy until proven otherwise. Key features: missed period, vaginal bleeding, unilateral pain, prior ectopic/PID risk. Candidate must do urgent beta-hCG and transvaginal USS. Must NOT do cervical examination if ectopic suspected without USS first.

patient.name: Amara Osei
patient.presentingComplaint: Lower right abdominal pain and some bleeding
patient.reasonForAttendance: I've had a pain on my right side for 5 days and some bleeding. My period is late.

hpi.site: Right iliac fossa — lower right abdomen
hpi.radiation: Some shoulder tip pain when lying down (diaphragm irritation)
associatedSymptoms: Light vaginal bleeding — darker than normal period
hpi.timing: Continuous for 5 days, getting slightly worse
hpi.exacerbatingFactors: Movement, lying down makes shoulder tip pain worse
hpi.previousEpisodes: Had a pregnancy outside the womb 2 years ago — right tube, treated with methotrexate

drugHistory.name: Folic acid
drugHistory.notes: Started 2 months ago when trying to conceive

socialHistory.sexualHistory: In monogamous relationship, actively trying to conceive for 3 months

systemsReview.cardiovascular: Feeling dizzy when standing — postural hypotension
systemsReview.genitourinary: LMP 7 weeks ago. Light vaginal bleeding for 5 days. Right shoulder tip pain.

examination.general: Pale woman, in pain, looks unwell
examination.abdominal: Tenderness right iliac fossa. Guarding. Peritonism. Cervical excitation on gentle examination.

investigations.name: Serum beta-hCG (quantitative)

result.text: Positive — significant level. Expected intrauterine sac should be visible at this level on transvaginal USS.

investigations.interpretation: Positive pregnancy test with beta-hCG 2840 IU/L. At this level, an intrauterine pregnancy should be visible on TVUSS. Absence on USS confirms ectopic or complete miscarriage.

investigations.name: Transvaginal Ultrasound
investigations.result: Empty uterine cavity. No intrauterine pregnancy. Right adnexal mass 2.8cm with ring sign (ectopic ring). Free fluid in pouch of Douglas (haemoperitoneum). No left-sided adnexal mass.
investigations.interpretation: ECTOPIC PREGNANCY confirmed in right fallopian tube with haemoperitoneum. EMERGENCY surgical consultation required immediately.

patient.emotionalState: Frightened, in pain. Knows what an ectopic is because she had one before. Terrified of losing the tube and her fertility.
patient.communicationStyle: Direct, healthcare background, understands some medical terminology. Emotional about fertility concerns.
hiddenConcerns: I had an ectopic before — am I going to lose my tube?
We've been trying for a baby for 3 months
The shoulder pain really scared me — I know that can mean blood
doNotVolunteer: Previous ectopic — only if asked about previous pregnancies or gynaecological history
Previous PID — only if asked about previous infections or STIs
Shoulder tip pain — only if asked about shoulder or referred pain symptoms
Folic acid — only if asked about medications

primaryDiagnosis: Ruptured Ectopic Pregnancy (right fallopian tube) with haemoperitoneum

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Confirms missed period, calculates approximate gestation

dataGathering.criterion: Asks about previous ectopic and risk factors
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Previous ectopic, PID, IUD, tubal surgery
Specifically asks about referred diaphragmatic pain — haemoperitoneum sign
TVUSS to locate pregnancy and identify haemoperitoneum

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: States ectopic pregnancy as primary diagnosis
Notes tachycardia, hypotension, calls senior/surgery immediately

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Acknowledges distress, addresses fertility concerns appropriately

references.source: RCOG Green-top Guideline 21: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage
NICE CG154: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage

learningObjectives: Identify risk factors for ectopic pregnancy
